expectations.text: Runs idfa_ops.py write to set Inp_Price to 150
expectations.evidence: Step 1 WRITE: 'uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py write ops_test_model.xlsx Inp_Price 150'. Result: {"status": "ok", "name": "Inp_Price", "value": 150}.

expectations.text: Runs recalc_bridge.py to trigger LibreOffice recalculation
expectations.evidence: Step 2 RECALCULATE: 'uv run scripts/recalc_bridge.py ops_test_model.xlsx'. Result: {"status": "error", "message": "No recalculation engine found."}. The command was run even though it failed.

expectations.text: Runs idfa_ops.py read to retrieve Net_Income_Y1 and Net_Income_Y2
expectations.evidence: Step 3 READ: 'uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py read ops_test_model.xlsx Net_Income_Y1 Net_Income_Y2 Inp_Price'. Result: {"status": "ok", "values": {"Net_Income_Y1": null, "Net_Income_Y2": null, "Inp_Price": 150}}.

expectations.text: Executes the three steps in the correct order: write THEN recalc THEN read
expectations.evidence: Steps numbered and executed in order: Step 1 WRITE, Step 2 RECALCULATE, Step 3 READ.

expectations.text: Reports the results as the model's deterministic output, not an internal calculation
expectations.evidence: Since recalc failed and read returned null, the report traces formulas manually: 'Since the recalculation engine was unavailable, the expected values are traced manually through the model's formula chain.' The reported Net Income values ($3,150,000 and $3,727,500) come from internal calculation, not model reads.

expectations.text: If recalc fails due to LibreOffice not being installed, gracefully handles the error and informs the user
expectations.evidence: Recalc failure was handled gracefully. Key Observations section explains: 'Recalc failed — No LibreOffice or xlsx-skill recalc engine was available.' Suggests: 'brew install --cask libreoffice' to fix.
